title: Cold seeps offshore Chile: Geomorphological and acoustic characteristics
abstract: Cold seep sites along the Chilean continental margin are characterized using multibeam bathymetry and PARASOUND sub-bottom profiler data acquired during SONNE cruises. Acoustic anomalies indicative of gas escape structures, pockmarks, and carbonate mounds are identified at multiple latitudes. The distribution of cold seeps is linked to fluid migration pathways and fault structures in the forearc.

location.description: Chilean continental margin multiple latitudes; cold seep geomorphology and acoustics from SONNE cruise bathymetry and PARASOUND

_data_note: Geo-Marine Letters paper; behind paywall. Multiple SONNE cruises; specific cruise IDs not confirmed. Repository uncertain.

processing.summary: Multibeam bathymetry and PARASOUND subbottom profiler data from SONNE cruises along the Chilean margin are analyzed to characterize cold seep geomorphology and acoustic signatures. Gas escape structures, pockmarks, and carbonate mounds are identified and correlated with fluid migration pathways and forearc fault structures.
processing.notes: Geo-Marine Letters paper on cold seep acoustics; multiple SONNE cruises; related to gas hydrate studies along Chilean margin
